summary: Judith Butler discusses the evolution of gender theory, distinguishing between assigned sex and gender as a cultural and psychic reality. They explain gender performativity as acts that bring reality into being, and argue that the current political assault on gender is fundamentally an assault on democracy and freedom.

principles.statement: The assault on gender is an assault on democracy.
principles.rationale: Gender freedom is an integral part of any democratic struggle. Restricting bodily autonomy and self-definition threatens the core democratic principles of equality, freedom, and justice.
principles.source_quote: the assault on gender is also an assault on democracy.

principles.statement: Gender is an open-ended question, not a final biological determination.
principles.rationale: While biological differences exist, they do not dictate how we must live. Gender is a mix of cultural norms, historical formations, and psychic realities that we have a say in.
principles.source_quote: I insist that what it is to be a woman, or indeed what it is to be a man or any other gender, is an open-ended question.

principles.statement: Assigned sex and taught gender should not determine your life path.
principles.rationale: People ought not to be discriminated against based on what they do with their bodies, who they love, or how they move and look.
principles.source_quote: All I was saying is that the sex you're assigned at birth and the gender that you are taught to be should not determine how you live your life.

principles.statement: Stumbling is a necessary part of learning new ways to respect others.
principles.rationale: People often resist using new language (like pronouns) because it is uncomfortable, but making errors is just part of adjusting habits to acknowledge people.
principles.source_quote: But stumbling is part of learning and making an error is part of learning, especially when we're learning something new.

mental_models.name: Gender Performativity
mental_models.description: Viewing gender not as an innate fact or fake performance, but as a 'performative' act that brings reality into being, similar to a judge declaring a couple married.
mental_models.example: When gay, lesbian, or trans people start living openly and speaking/acting in certain ways, they enact their gender anew and literally change reality and societal terms.

mental_models.name: The Widened Lens of Oppression
mental_models.description: Understanding that oppressive regimes target multiple marginalized groups simultaneously, meaning justice must be sought for all who suffer similarly, not just one's own group.
mental_models.example: Realizing that the Nazi regime extinguished not just Jews, but queer people, disabled people, and communists, which necessitates a broader pursuit of justice.

heuristics: Allow yourself to be challenged and revise your thinking to hear others.
Stumbling and making errors is part of learning new language.

signature_quotes.text: the assault on gender is also an assault on democracy.
signature_quotes.context: Explaining why they are less concerned with whose gender theory is right, and more concerned with the political attacks on gender.

signature_quotes.text: I insist that what it is to be a woman, or indeed what it is to be a man or any other gender, is an open-ended question.
signature_quotes.context: Discussing the core premise of their work on gender.

signature_quotes.text: when we talk about performative, we're talking about an act that makes something come into being or an act that has real consequences.
signature_quotes.context: Clarifying the definition of 'performative' in the context of gender.

signature_quotes.text: My whole life, I've wanted to take a break from gender. I can never take a break from gender.
signature_quotes.context: Joking with the crew at the end of the interview.

anti_patterns.description: Treating gender as a fixed, natural fact.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It ignores the cultural and psychic realities that shape gender. People cling to this because allowing others to define themselves destabilizes their own rigid self-definition, which they find frightening.

anti_patterns.description: Defining feminism solely around maternity or presumptive heterosexuality.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It excludes people based on what they do with their bodies or who they love, failing to recognize that sex assigned at birth shouldn't determine how one lives.

anti_patterns.description: Retreating into vitriol and absolute moral certainty.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: If we only scream and act as the 'moral center of our universe,' we prevent conversation and fail to hear marginalized people making claims to be acknowledged.
